2604.19514.title: When Graph Structure Becomes a Liability: A Critical Re-Evaluation of Graph Neural Networks for Bitcoin Fraud Detection under Temporal Distribution Shift
2604.19514.paper_summary: The consensus that GCN, GraphSAGE, GAT, and EvolveGCN outperform feature-only baselines on the Elliptic Bitcoin Dataset is widely cited but has not been rigorously stress-tested under a leakage-free evaluation protocol. We perform a seed-matched inductive-versus-transductive comparison and find that this consensus does not hold. Under a strictly inductive protocol, Random Forest on raw features achieves F1 = 0.821 and outperforms all evaluated GNNs, while GraphSAGE reaches F1 = 0.689 +/- 0.017. A paired controlled experiment reveals a 39.5-point F1 gap attributable to training-time exposure to test-period adjacency. Additionally, edge-shuffle ablations show that randomly wired graphs outperform the real transaction graph, indicating that the dataset's topology can be misleading under temporal distribution shift. Hybrid models combining GNN embeddings with raw features provide only marginal gains and remain substantially below feature-only baselines. We release code, checkpoints, and a strict-inductive protocol to enable reproducible, leakage-free evaluation.
2604.19514.paper_summary_zh: 廣泛引用的共識認為 GCN、GraphSAGE、GAT 和 EvolveGCN 在 Elliptic Bitcoin Dataset 上的表現優於僅使用特徵的基準，但在無泄漏的評估協議下尚未經過嚴格的壓力測試。我們進行了種子匹配的歸納與傳導比較，發現這一共識並不成立。在嚴格的歸納協議下，隨機森林在原始特徵上的 F1 值達到 0.821，並超越了所有評估的 GNN，而 GraphSAGE 的 F1 值為 0.689 +/- 0.017。一項配對控制實驗顯示，由於訓練期間對測試期間鄰接的暴露，導致了 39.5 點的 F1 差距。此外，邊緣隨機重排的消融實驗顯示，隨機連接的圖形表現優於真實交易圖，這表明在時間分佈變化下，數據集的拓撲可能會誤導。結合 GNN 嵌入和原始特徵的混合模型僅提供了邊際增益，並且仍然顯著低於僅使用特徵的基準。我們發布了代碼、檢查點和嚴格的歸納協議，以實現可重複的、無泄漏的評估。
2604.19514.author: Saket Maganti et.al.
